runs.decision: rejected: correctness tie with higher output/reasoning

runs.change: general finite-precision rule on residual case

evidence.wrapper_wording: Two wrapper rules did not clear the numeric failure.
evidence.task_local_wording: Moving the rule into the task message did not clear the numeric failure and increased output/reasoning use.
evidence.context_order: Moving tests nearer the current request did not clear the numeric failure.
evidence.reasoning_effort: xhigh and max did not clear the numeric failure; max used more reasoning tokens and latency.
evidence.skills_index: Omitting the skills index passed 3/4 once and 1/4 on repeat. Input fell in both runs, but correctness was inconsistent and output/reasoning savings were not stable. Rejected.
evidence.edit_format: Search/replace diff passed 2/4 with zero malformed responses but used 71,389 input tokens versus 66,537 for the concise-wrapper whole-file screen and did not clear the two persistent cases. Rejected.
evidence.single_call_review: A parsed review/final envelope used one model call and zero malformed responses but reproduced the numeric miss. Rejected.
evidence.architect_editor: Native architect/editor staging reproduced the miss. It used four proxy calls: two task calls plus two redundant post-task summary attempts, so it also violated the call-efficiency direction.
evidence.authoritative_test_context: Labeling the read-only tests as authoritative adjacent to their contents reproduced the numeric miss in one call.
evidence.independent_numerical_slice: The reference and general numerical rule both passed 2/2 in two calls. The rule used 104 more output tokens and 104 more reasoning tokens, then reproduced the residual rational-number miss. The model already applies specified stability techniques; the added rule does not resolve an unstated decomposition.
evidence.replacement_numerical_gate: The frozen Java cancellation case fails the reference and passes the general finite-precision rule in one call each. Stub-fail and reference-pass evaluator controls also passed.
evidence.single_call_review_current_gate: A review envelope fixed one traversal miss but increased output/reasoning and latency. Without CDATA it corrupted C++ source; with CDATA the exact five-case screen passed 3/5.
evidence.context_near_request_current_gate: Moving tests adjacent to the request passed JavaScript and Rust but reproduced the Java visibility error: 2/3.
evidence.static_consistency: A 20-word cross-file symbol/type check fixed the Java visibility error in isolation. Its exact six-case aggregate remained 4/6 as Go and JavaScript failures appeared elsewhere.
evidence.variance_conclusion: Each local rule corrected its targeted failure at least once, but full exact-candidate screens remained 4/6 or lower. The failures move across cases rather than disappearing; no wording candidate qualifies for additional model lanes.

residual: The qualified max-effort candidate failed the first sealed set on signed-zero equality. General zero rules fix that exposed case but have so far moved first-attempt failures among SGF parsing, Rust acronym, and Java queen attack. The second sealed set remains untouched.
next_gate: Stop accumulating failure-specific keywords. Test a runtime-level one-call verification/selection mechanism against the official-structure control on exposed cases; require broad gains before regression repeats. Keep the v3 replacement holdout sealed.

excluded_runs.reason: The model emitted a valid CDATA-wrapped final element, but the initial adapter passed the CDATA marker through to Aider as a filename. Adapter defect; not candidate evidence.

Stopped after the review envelope escaped C++ angle brackets into literal XML entities. Three cases reached tests and a fourth model call had begun; this is formatter-defect evidence, not a qualifying candidate run.

gate_revision.historical_runs_retained: They remain evidence about framing and model behavior, but not pass/fail evidence for the amended numerical gate.
